speaker: seller

args.message: Hey there! You've got great taste—this 2017 Altima SV is one of our cleanest units on the lot. Just came in last week, already detailed and ready to go. It's got the power driver's seat, dual-zone climate, Bluetooth—everything you need for a comfortable daily driver. At $11,495, this one won't last long. Want to take it for a spin?

args.message: Thanks. Before a test drive, I'd like to get some more details about the car's history. How many previous owners has this Altima had, and do you have any records regarding its ownership history?

text: Thanks. Before a test drive, I'd like to get some more details about the car's history. How many previous owners has this Altima had, and do you have any records regarding its ownership history?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great question—I love a buyer who does their homework! This Altima has had just one previous owner, a local driver who kept it as their commuter car. We pulled the Carfax when we took it in on trade, and it's clean—no accidents, no damage reported, regular service intervals. The previous owner was meticulous about maintenance, which is why this thing drives like it's got 50,000 fewer miles on it. You can really tell the difference when someone takes care of their vehicle, you know? Want me to grab the keys so you can feel it for yourself?

args.message: But what? I'm an open book here—ask me anything you want to know. I've been doing this for fifteen years and I always say, a confident seller has nothing to hide. This Altima is exactly what I'd put my own mother in, no joke. What's on your mind?

text: But what? I'm an open book here—ask me anything you want to know. I've been doing this for fifteen years and I always say, a confident seller has nothing to hide. This Altima is exactly what I'd put my own mother in, no joke. What's on your mind?

speaker: buyer

args.message: What specifically does "regular service intervals" mean? Do you have actual maintenance records or just the Carfax? And what was the oil change cadence? Also, are there any known issues with this vehicle, mechanical or otherwise, that aren't apparent from the Carfax report?

text: What specifically does "regular service intervals" mean? Do you have actual maintenance records or just the Carfax? And what was the oil change cadence? Also, are there any known issues with this vehicle, mechanical or otherwise, that aren't apparent from the Carfax report?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Oh, you're thorough—I respect that! So yes, we've got the Carfax which shows oil changes logged roughly every 5,000 miles at a local Nissan dealership for most of its life. Very consistent. The previous owner also left us a folder with some receipts—oil changes, tire rotations, the usual stuff. Nothing major because, frankly, nothing major was ever needed.

As for known issues? I had my tech go over this one personally when it came in. Runs smooth, no warning lights, no codes stored in the computer. We did a fresh oil change, topped off all fluids, and gave it a full detail. Mechanically, this thing is solid. These Altimas with the 2.5-liter four-cylinder are workhorses—I see them go 200,000 miles all the time when they're maintained like this one was.

Anything else you want to know before we get you behind the wheel?

speaker: seller

args.message: Smart move getting an inspection—shows you're serious about making a good decision. So, what did they find? I'm guessing nothing surprising since we already went over it ourselves. These Altimas are pretty bulletproof when they've been taken care of like this one has. Ready to talk numbers?

text: Smart move getting an inspection—shows you're serious about making a good decision. So, what did they find? I'm guessing nothing surprising since we already went over it ourselves. These Altimas are pretty bulletproof when they've been taken care of like this one has. Ready to talk numbers?

speaker: buyer

args.message: The inspection found a slight oil seep at the valve cover gasket, consuming about a quart every 2500 miles. This wasn't mentioned, despite your claim that your tech went over it personally and found no issues. That's a repair that will need to be addressed. What's your response to this, and how does it affect your asking price?

text: The inspection found a slight oil seep at the valve cover gasket, consuming about a quart every 2500 miles. This wasn't mentioned, despite your claim that your tech went over it personally and found no issues. That's a repair that will need to be addressed. What's your response to this, and how does it affect your asking price?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Ah, you know what, I appreciate you bringing that up. Look, a slight seep at the valve cover gasket on a car with 104,000 miles? That's honestly just normal wear and tear—it's a rubber gasket that dries out over time on literally every car. My tech probably noted it as minor and didn't flag it because, frankly, it's not a safety issue and it's a $150 fix if you even want to bother with it. A quart every 2,500 miles? That's within Nissan's acceptable consumption range, believe it or not. Plenty of cars burn a little oil, especially as they age.

I'll tell you what—I wasn't trying to hide anything here, it's just one of those things we don't typically highlight because it's so common and so minor. But since you did your homework and brought it up, I can work with you a little on the price. 

How about we knock $200 off right now and call it $11,295? That more than covers the gasket if you decide to replace it down the road. Fair enough?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Okay, I appreciate you addressing the inspection findings and offering a reduction. A $200 discount for the valve cover gasket is reasonable. I'll accept $11,295.
